title: Pass 5.C.1: ferrotorch-train checkpoint_sequential is straight-through forward (lies about checkpointing)
description:
Pass 4 finding (umbrella #982) — ferrotorch-train audit, STUB+DOC_DRIFT.

src/checkpoint.rs:101-119 — checkpoint_sequential 'if requires_grad' branch is identical to else branch; no checkpoint call, no RNG save, no recompute boundary. Comment block at lines 84-110 admits 'we run the forward pass directly', 'we cannot capture modules'. Function is a straight-through forward dressed up as gradient checkpointing.

Module doc at src/checkpoint.rs:13-18 claims 'GPU RNG state is saved and restored' and 'Nested calls to checkpoint are supported' — both false for the only fn defined here. lib.rs:54-56 table makes the same claim for the user-facing surface.

Acceptance:
- Implement real checkpointing (RNG save + recompute on backward) routing through ferrotorch-core's checkpoint primitive, OR
- Remove checkpoint_sequential entirely + amend doc to point at the correct primitive
- Discriminating test that fails if backward doesn't trigger recompute (peak-mem assertion or grad-fn-trace assertion).
